guide-text: Ecosystem - Flora A living ecosystem layer for Vintage Story. Wild plants spread, compete, and respond to their environment — all using vanilla blocks. Plants in the ecosystem • Meadow flowers (20 species) — daisies, cornflowers, poppies, catmint, heather, and more • Tallgrass — fills in as a grass matrix under flowers • Ferns (5 species) — forest understory; prefer shade and moisture • Berry bushes (10 wild species) — soil and forest edge preference • Trees (14 species) — mature trunks seed free saplings nearby • Reeds — cooper's reed and papyrus on gravel beds near water • Water lily — spreads across open water surfaces • Water crowfoot — underwater column plant, 2–8 blocks deep How spreading works Mature plants periodically try to reproduce onto a nearby cell. Whether it succeeds depends on: • Climate — temperature and rainfall • Soil — fertility and type (forest floor, peat, sand, clay, gravel) • Light and moisture — shade under canopy, proximity to water • Forest density — counted from nearby tree trunks • Spacing — plants respect distance from same and other species Competition Plants don't just fill empty space — they compete. A stronger species can displace a weaker one from an occupied cell. Plants stuck in the wrong niche (too dry, wrong soil, not enough light) accumulate stress and eventually die. Symbiosis Forest understory — ferns, lily of the valley, some berries — depends on nearby trees. Cut the tree, and its understory gradually withers. Seasons • Spring — boosted spread; meadows bloom early • Summer — steady growth • Fall — annual flowers die off; perennials slow down • Winter — most plants go dormant; annuals die, hardy species survive Land claims Wild spread, displacement, and stress death are blocked inside land claims. Your gardens and farms stay safe. Configuration Set BalancePreset to natural (default), lush, or sparse to tune spread intensity. All features — seasons, competition, symbiosis, niche stress — can be toggled individually in the config file. Removing the mod Every block placed by the mod is a vanilla game block. Removing the mod leaves your world completely intact.
